item.description: CTF Writeups Reverse Engineering (Rev) 1.GateKeeper 2.Hidden Cipher 1 3.Hidden Cipher 2 4.Bypassme 5.The add-on trap 6.Silent Stream 7.Auto rev 1 8.Secure password DB 9.Bin ins 3 10.Bin Ins 4 11.JIFTP Inex 1.QuizPlot 2.Echo Escape 1 3.Echo Escape 2 4.Teacash 5.Heap Havoc 6.Offset Cycle 7.Offset Cycl...

item.title: 8. Pizza Router
item.description: Given Map Overview Map Overview We are given three maps: city1, city2, city3. Legend: S → start .

item.title: 10. Bin Ins 4
item.description: File Quick check w/ strings No meaningful strings found Output mostly looks like gibberish Ghidra - entry (bin-ins.exe) Explanation From the entry function, we don’t see any clear application logic Instead, it mostly: allocates memory stores pointers / prepares buffers performs a lot of setup-like ...

item.title: 9. Bin ins 3
item.description: File Quick check w/ strings No meaningful strings found Output mostly looks like gibberish Ghidra - entry ( bin-ins.exe ) Explanation From the entry function, we don’t see any clear application logic Instead, it mostly: allocates memory stores pointers / prepares buffers performs a lot of setup-lik...

item.title: 8. Secure password DB
item.description: Given files: system.out Quick check Quick Check (strings -n 4 system.out) From the visible strings, the program flow seems to be: set a password provide its length then enter its hash to access the account At this stage, we still do not know how the hash is generated or what condition leads to the ...

item.title: 7. Auto rev 1
item.description: Observation At first, I thought the value shown at the top was some kind of seed My initial idea was: reverse the binary use that seed to recover the secret But that interpretation turned out to be wrong The key clue is the wording: “Here’s the next binary in bytes:” That means the binary dump belo...

item.title: 6. Silent Stream
item.description: Given files: encrypt.py packets.pcap Observation packets.pcap is a packet capture file it stores recorded network traffic in this case, likely the captured file transfer We also get encrypt.py this shows how the file was processed before being sent so it gives us the encoding/encryption logic used ...

item.title: 5. The add-on trap
item.description: Initial Setup Given file: .xpi add-on archive Extracted it using password: picoctf After extraction, contents were placed into: extracted_addon Cd extracted_addon Explanation After extracting, we inspect the contents of extracted_addon/ The most important file here is manifest.json Why? In browser ...

item.title: 4. Bypassme
item.description: File Initial Observation After a quick initial check ( strings -n 4 bypassme.bin ) , nothing immediately revealed the password Running the binary shows this is clearly an authentication challenge So the next step is to inspect the login logic with LLDB and recover / bypass the password check LLDB E...
